source.title: Accident Investigation Report - South Pass 62 C Platform Gas Release

context.region: Gulf of America, South Pass Block 62

actions_taken: Platform manually shut in after gas release confirmed
Portable gas detector used to confirm gas leak
G-111 C Component Shut in Enforcement INC issued
Platform shut in from 03 November to 09 November 2025
INC correction notification submitted with repair photographs on 10 November 2025
event.summary: On 03 November 2025 at 1205 hours, a BSEE Inspector conducting an annual production inspection on South Pass 62 C platform noticed suspected gas fumes coming from a corroded area of an 8-inch gas vent line. Platform operators confirmed the gas release using a portable gas detector. The gas was leaking from the corroded pipe and blowing under the wet and dry oil tanks. The platform was manually shut in. The gas release was not detected by the platform safety system and did not initiate any alarms. The corroded section of piping had not been identified by API Level 1 surveys or platform operators. No injuries or pollution occurred. The platform remained shut in until repairs were completed on 09 November 2025.
recommendations: BSEE New Orleans District recommends the Office of Incident Investigations should consider issuing a Safety Alert regarding the incident
Further attention should be given to identifying corrosion issues on all platform process piping
Operator should consider establishing routine corrosion inspections in addition to the Level 1 surveys
Additional efforts in identifying corrosion on process piping could prevent future gas releases and limit the possibility of explosion hazards
key_phrases: gas release not detected by platform safety system
corroded area of the 8-inch gas vent line where the gas release occurred was not identified on the level 1 surveys
operators were unaware of the on-going hazard
more severe incident could have occurred if an ignition source had been introduced

threats.name: Failure to identify corrosion during inspections

controls.name: API Level 1 Survey (Third-party inspection)
controls.lod_basis: Periodic inspection program to identify degradation

supporting_text: Review of the Level 1 surveys also revealed that the corroded area of the 8-inch gas vent line where the gas release occurred was not identified on the level 1 surveys
Equipment Failure: Inadequate equipment inspection- Operators and third-party inspectors failed to identify the corroded area of the 8-inch gas vent line
The affected 8-inch gas pipe was not listed on the most recent Level 1 Surveys

controls.name: Operator routine corrosion inspections
controls.barrier_role: Ongoing corrosion monitoring by platform operators

supporting_text: Due to the corroded section of piping not being identified by the Level 1 surveys or by platform operators, the operators were unaware of the on-going hazard
Equipment Failure: Inadequate equipment inspection- Operators and third-party inspectors failed to identify the corroded area of the 8-inch gas vent line
The Operator should consider establishing routine corrosion inspections in addition to the Level 1 surveys

controls.name: Preventive maintenance program for corrosion control

supporting_text: Equipment Failure: Inadequate equipment maintenance- Operators failed to perform necessary maintenance to prevent corrosion on production piping

controls.name: Platform gas detection system
controls.barrier_role: Detect gas release and initiate alarms
controls.lod_basis: Automated detection system to alert operators

supporting_text: There was no muster associated with this event, as the gas release was not detected by the platform safety system, and did not initiate any alarms
While observing the area, the BSEE Inspector thought he saw vapors coming from piping located on the platform level above his location
After testing the LBPs, the Inspector went up to the level above and saw the vapors coming from a corroded area on an 8-inch gas vent line
After noticing the suspected gas vapors, the Inspector notified the Person in Charge (PIC) of the platform to investigate the vapors

controls.name: Portable gas detector verification
controls.barrier_role: Confirm gas release and support decision-making
controls.lod_basis: Secondary detection method to confirm hazard

supporting_text: The PIC then retrieved a portable gas detector from the PIC's office to check the area for a gas leak and confirmed gas to be leaking out of the pipe
The platform operators then used a gas detector to verify the inspector's suspicions and confirmed that there was a gas release from the corroded area of the pipe

controls.name: Manual platform shutdown
controls.barrier_role: Stop gas release and prevent escalation
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response action to control incident

supporting_text: After confirming the gas release, the platform was manually shut in
After confirming the release, the platform was shut in to prevent any further gas release

controls.name: Ignition source control
controls.lod_basis: Control of potential ignition sources in hazardous areas

supporting_text: A more severe incident could have occurred if an ignition source had been introduced to the area of the unidentified gas release
Luckily, there were no known ignition sources near the gas release while ongoing

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
